title: Don't use `module mod_name(...)` style in cell libs
body:
_What are the reasons/motivation for this change?_

close #5637 

_Explain how this is achieved._

Add `fix_mod.py` which loads a cell lib in Yosys, calls `write_verilog`, and then replaces the original module declaration with the one from Yosys (which lists the port names).  Run the script on each cell lib that contains `module mod_name(...)` style declarations to minimize friction when using them as blackbox definitions in non-Yosys tooling (e.g. Verilator for linting with `--bbox-unsup`).

This does not affect files with techmap definitions such as `*/lutrams_map.v`, since those are intended for internal Yosys use only (converting internal, temporary cell types into proper hardware supported cell types), and do not need to be compatible with external tools.
